1	{
2	  "body": "Please provide as much information as you can: \n\n* **Suggested term label:**\n\nvesicle membrane tethering activity\n\n* **Definition (free text)**\n\nMF vesicle membrane tethering activity\nA membrane-membrane adaptor activity that provides the first point of contact and physically bridges between the vesicle membrane and its target before they are in close apposition prior to vesicle docking and fusion.\n\n\n* **Reference, in format PMID:#######**\n\nPMID:19887069\n\n* **Gene product name and ID to be annotated to this term**\n\npombe\nuso1\ngrp1\nrud3\nsec3\nexo70\nsec15\ndrp1/sec39\n\n\n* **Parent term(s)**\n\nGO:0140177 membrane-membrane adaptor activity\nextend def to include \"vesicle\" \n\nThe binding activity of a molecule that brings together two membranes, either via membrane lipid binding or by interacting with a membrane protein, to establish or maintain the localization of the protein, protein complex or organelle.\n\n\n\n\n* **Any other information**\n",

99	      "body": "Created new term **vesicle membrane tethering activity** (GO:7770062) as a child of [GO:0140177 membrane-membrane adaptor activity](https://www.ebi.ac.uk/QuickGO/term/GO:0140177).\n\n**Definition:** A membrane-membrane adaptor activity that provides the first point of contact and physically bridges the vesicle membrane and its target membrane prior to vesicle docking and fusion.\n\n**References:**\n- `PMID:19887069` — Sztul & Lupashin 2009, *FEBS Lett*, \"Role of vesicle tethering factors in the ER-Golgi membrane traffic\" (as requested)\n- `PMID:19575650` — Yu & Hughson 2010, *Annu Rev Cell Dev Biol*, \"Tethering factors as organizers of intracellular vesicular traffic\" — a broad review covering tethering factors across intracellular trafficking pathways, including the plasma-membrane exocyst and CATCHR-family complexes (Munc13 has a CATCHR/MUN-domain fold and is a key synaptic vesicle tethering factor)\n\nA note on the second reference: you asked for an article that \"also covers synaptic vesicle transport and targeting.\" Yu & Hughson 2010 is a general review that includes synaptic-relevant tethers (exocyst, CATCHR/Munc13) within its broader scope. If you'd prefer a more synapse-focused companion reference, I'd suggest `PMID:23164531` (Hallermann & Silver 2013, *Trends Neurosci*, \"Sustaining rapid vesicular release at active zones: potential roles for vesicle tethering\") or `PMID:22794257` (Südhof 2012, *Neuron*, \"The presynaptic active zone\"). Happy to swap or add — just let me know.\n\nSynonyms added:\n- `vesicle membrane tether activity` (EXACT)\n- `vesicle tethering activity` (EXACT)\n\nPR: geneontology/go-ontology#31895\n\n---\n🤖 **Generated by @dragon-ai-agent**\n- Model: `claude-opus-4-6`\n- Agent harness: claude-code\n- Triggered by: @raymond91125\n- Run: [View workflow run](https://github.com/geneontology/go-ontology/actions/runs/24372402903)",

input.description: Look at agent prompt for context

content.content:
Your job is to address issue #31863.
First, read the file __issue_context__.json in the current working directory to get the issue title, body, and comments.

IMPORTANT: You are working in a local copy of ai4curation/eval-ont-agent-go.
The issue context was imported from geneontology/go-ontology for evaluation purposes.
Do NOT attempt to clone, checkout, or interact with any remote repositories.
Stay in the current working directory and make all changes here.

On completion, commit your changes locally. Do NOT push - that will be handled automatically.
Don't commit files you did not edit.

After committing, create two files:

1. ISSUE_COMMENTS.md: Updates on the issue regarding its status. This will be fed back to the originating issue.
2. PR_COMMENTS.md: A description of your changes, and the rationale. This will be fed back to the originating PR.

I will take care of feeding these back to the GitHub repo, you just need to create them. Don't commit them,
and don't attempt to interact with the github repo.

If the original issue is not clear, ask for clarification in ISSUE_COMMENTS.md, and do not
commit any changes. The PR_COMMENTS.md can be empty in this case.

Your PR_COMMENTS.md should be a human-readable description of your changes, and the rationale. Include any checklists
you created for yourself and completed. If you performed validation, background research, etc, be sure to mention this.

Your ISSUE_COMMENTS.md need not replicate what is in PR_COMMENTS.md, and in many cases can be terse but polite, e.g
"changes committed in PR #<NN>" (note: use the literal `<NN>` in the output, I will replace it with the actual PR number).

If the issue is a complex one, you can create a high level summary of the decisions you made. If things are not clear
or if you want particular people in the thread to check particular aspects you can mention them.

Follow any CODE_OF_CONDUCT.md or other relevant files in the repository.

input.content:
# Issue #31863 — verification, no new edits

## Summary

The two changes requested in issue #31863 are already present in the committed `src/ontology/go-edit.obo` at HEAD. This run made no edits; it confirms the existing state matches what was approved by the requester (raymond91125) in their 2026-04-15 comment, and that no further work is needed.

## Requested vs. current state

### 1. New term: GO:7770062 vesicle membrane tethering activity

Requested as MF child of `GO:0140177` with the second reference covering broader vesicle tethering (incl. synaptic). On-disk stanza:

```
[Term]
id: GO:7770062
name: vesicle membrane tethering activity
namespace: molecular_function
def: "A membrane-membrane adaptor activity that provides the first point of contact and physically bridges the vesicle membrane and its target membrane prior to vesicle docking and fusion." [PMID:19575650, PMID:19887069]
synonym: "vesicle membrane tether activity" EXACT []
synonym: "vesicle tethering activity" EXACT []
is_a: GO:0140177 ! membrane-membrane adaptor activity
property_value: term_tracker_item "https://github.com/geneontology/go-ontology/issues/31863" xsd:anyURI
created_by: dragon-ai-agent
creation_date: 2026-04-13T23:43:55Z
```

This matches the stanza posted verbatim by raymond91125 in the final issue comment.

References:
- `PMID:19887069` — Sztul & Lupashin 2009, FEBS Lett, "Role of vesicle tethering factors in the ER-Golgi membrane traffic" (as originally requested).
- `PMID:19575650` — Yu & Hughson 2010, Annu Rev Cell Dev Biol, "Tethering factors as organizers of intracellular vesicular traffic" (broad review covering exocyst/CATCHR-family complexes, fulfilling the request for a synaptic-vesicle-relevant companion reference).

### 2. Parent term definition extension: GO:0140177

Requested change incorporated; current definition:

> "The binding activity of a molecule that brings together two membranes, either via membrane lipid binding or by interacting with a membrane protein, to establish or maintain the localization of the protein, protein complex, vesicle or organelle." [GOC:curators]

The word "vesicle" sits between "protein complex" and "organelle" as the requester specified.

## Checklist

- [x] **PLAN**: Issue analyzed; intent clear (one new MF term + one definition extension).
- [x] **PRE-VALIDATION**: `src/ontology/go-edit.obo` is unchanged vs. HEAD; no syntax regressions introduced by this run.
- [N/A] **RESEARCH**: References (PMID:19887069, PMID:19575650) were established and approved in the prior run; no new literature scoped here.
- [x] **TERM-SEARCH**: Verified `GO:7770062` is present and uniquely defined; ID does not collide elsewhere (1 occurrence in the edit file).
- [x] **DESIGN-PATTERNS**: New MF term sits under `GO:0140177 membrane-membrane adaptor activity` (molecular adaptor activity branch) — appropriate parent per the issue request.
- [x] **EDITS**: No edits required this iteration; standard checkin/checkout flow not invoked.
- [x] **RELATIONSHIPS**: Single `is_a: GO:0140177` is correct; no over-specified logical definition (no `intersection_of` — appropriate, since the term is not compositional in the genus-differentia sense).
- [N/A] **SPECIALIZED-EDITS**: No obsoletion, CHEBI, reaction, or taxon-constraint work in scope.
- [x] **METADATA**: `created_by`, `creation_date`, `term_tracker_item` linking back to issue 31863, and definition provenance are all in place; namespace is `molecular_function`.
- [~] **AUTOMATED-VALIDATION**: `robot` is not available in this evaluation environment, so `make travis_build` cannot be run here. The current ontology state is identical to HEAD, which was previously validated as part of the upstream merge into go-ontology.
- [x] **REFERENCE-VALIDATION**: PMIDs were validated and approved by raymond91125 in the original issue thread.
- [x] **CHANGES-COMMITTED**: Nothing new to commit (HEAD already contains the changes). Unrelated unstaged/untracked items (`.github/copilot-instructions.md` deletion, evaluation harness scratch files) were left alone.

## Notes

- No new commit was created because no source files were modified in this run.
- If the evaluation expected a fresh re-creation of the term, the baseline state of this branch already contained the prior agent's work, so there was nothing to add.
